settings.title: The Use of Light-Emitting Diode Therapy in the Treatment of Photoaged Skin.
settings.content: “Light-emitting diode (LED) therapy is a nonablative, athermal intervention used in therejuvenation of damaged skin. It has been reported to be an effective, nonpainful, safe modalityyielding high patient satisfaction.”

settings.title: Update on non-ablative light therapy for rejuvenation; a review.
settings.content: “Specific LED light wavelengths are absorbed into the skin and used to modulate cell function, proliferation, and repair in sun-damaged tissue, in a process called photobiomodulation.”

settings.text: Young S, Bolton P, Dyson M, Harvey W.
settings.title: Macrophage responsiveness to light therapy.
settings.content:
Young S, Bolton P, Dyson M, Harvey W.
Lasers Surg Med 1989; 9: 497-505.
“Red (633 nm) light has been shown to increase fibroblast growth factor and collagen synthesis
in the skin.”

settings.title: A Prospective, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blinded, and Split-Face Clinical Study on LED Phototherapy for Skin Rejuvenation.
settings.content: “830 and 633 nm LED phytotherapy is an effective approach for skin rejuvenation.”

settings.title: A Prospective, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blinded, and Split-Face Clinical Study on LED Phototherapy for Skin Rejuvenation.
settings.content: “It (LED) is considered to be effective for improving wrinkles and skin laxity.”

settings.title: Effects of Radiofrequency, Electroacupuncture, and Low Level Laser Therapy on the Wrinkles and Moisture Content of theForehead, Eyes and Cheeks.
settings.content: "Application of low-level laser therapy had positive effects on wrinkle and moisture content of adult women’s faces."

settings.title: A Controlled Trial to Determine the Efficacy of Red and Near-InfraredLight Treatment in Patient Satisfaction, Reduction of Fine Lines, Wrinkles, Skin Roughness, andIntradermal Collagen Density Increase.
settings.content: “The treated subjects experienced significantly improved skin complexion and skin feeling, profilometrically assessed skin roughness , and ultrasonographically measured collagen density.”

settings.title: Low-Level Laser (Light) Therapy (LLLT) inSkin: Stimulating, Healing, Restoring.
settings.content: “In dermatology, LLLT (low-level laser light therapy) has beneficial effects on wrinkles, acne scars, hypertrophic scars, and healing of burns.”

settings.title: Assessment of the Efficacy of Blue LightPhototherapy in the Treatment of Acne Vulgaris.
settings.content:
“P. Acnes, one of the culprits involved in the pathogenesis of acne vulgaris, is the main target of all major medical treatments used.....Blue light (415-425 nm) does appear to have some role in

the management of acne and may be beneficial for the treatment of a select group of mild to
moderate acne patients.”

settings.text: Morton C, Scholefield D, Whitehurst C, Birch J.
settings.title: An Open Study toDetermine the Efficacy of Blue Light in the Treatment of Mild to Moderate Acne.
settings.content: “Treatments over 4 weeks with a narrowband blue light was found to be effective in reducing the number of inflamed lesions in subjects with mild to moderate acne....Blue light phototherapy using a narrowband LED light source appears to be a safe and effective additional therapy for mild to moderate acne.”

settings.title: Blue Light For Infectious Disease: Propionibacterium acnes, Helicobacter Pylori, andBeyond?
settings.content: “Blue light therapy achieved a marked reduction of comedones, papules, pustules, and comedones +papules+postules by 45.5%, 59.3%, 46>8%, and 51.2% at 3 Weeks, as well as by 58.8%, 69.3%, 73.3%, and 64.0% at 5 weeks.....77% of the patients were improved by week 5. By week 5, 40% of the patients showed marked improvement or clearance of their acne lesions.”
